2026-04-09T16:36:55.6246665Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-09T16:34:39.949936727Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58005,\"title\":\"[MySql Provisioning] Docs: README missing authentication, examples, and key concepts for initial beta release\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.MySql`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (version increment after release PR #57985) by `@azure-sdk`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\n`Azure.Provisioning.MySql` was published as its initial **1.0.0-beta.1** release on 2026-04-09. The package provides declarative provisioning support for Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Servers and includes the following generated resource types:\\n\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServer`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServerBackup` / `MySqlFlexibleServerBackupV2`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance`\\n- `AdvancedThreatProtection`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServersPrivateEndpointConnection`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServersCapability`\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **\\u0026#34;Authenticate the Client\\u0026#34; section is empty** — no content; comparable packages like `Azure.Provisioning.PostgreSql` also leave this blank, but for a first release targeting developers it should at minimum note that no client authentication is required (Bicep/ARM output is passed to `azd`)\\n- **\\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section is empty** — no code showing how to create a `MySqlFlexibleServer`; the analogous `Azure.Provisioning.PostgreSql` README includes a full working example with SKU, version, admin credentials, storage, backup, and high availability configuration\\n- **\\u0026#34;Next steps\\u0026#34; section is empty** — no links to Azure MySQL documentation, quickstart templates, or related packages\\n- **No sample test file** — `tests/` directory contains only the `.csproj`; there is no `Samples/` subdirectory with a snippet-backed test, so the README examples cannot be kept in sync with the snippet tooling\\n- **Key concepts section lacks resource inventory** — does not mention which resource types the package exposes or their relationships (e.g., `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase` and `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule` are child resources of `MySqlFlexibleServer`)\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md` — edit\\n\\nReplace the empty `### Authenticate the Client`, `## Key concepts`, `## Examples`, and `## Next steps` sections with the content shown below.\\n\\n**\\u0026#34;Authenticate the Client\\u0026#34; section** — replace the empty heading block with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Authenticate the Client\\n\\nNo runtime client authentication is needed for this library. `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` generates Bicep/ARM templates that are deployed to Azure using the [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) Credentials are handled at deployment time by `azd`, not by this library.\\n```\\n\\n**\\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section** — replace the existing paragraph with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThis library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using .NET. You can then use `azd` to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain Bicep or ARM templates.\\n\\n### Available resource types\\n\\n| Type | ARM resource |\\n|------|-------------|\\n| `MySqlFlexibleServer` | `Microsoft.DBforMySQL/flexibleServers` |\\n| `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase` | `.../databases` |\\n| `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule` | `.../firewallRules` |\\n| `MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator` | `.../administrators` |\\n| `MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration` | `.../configurations` |\\n| `MySqlFlexibleServerBackup` / `MySqlFlexibleServerBackupV2` | `.../backups` |\\n| `MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance` | `.../maintenances` |\\n| `AdvancedThreatProtection` | `.../advancedThreatProtectionSettings` |\\n| `MySqlFlexibleServersPrivateEndpointConnection` | `.../privateEndpointConnections` |\\n```\\n\\n**\\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section** — replace the empty heading with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Create a MySQL Flexible Server\\n\\n```\\nInfrastructure infra = new();\\n\\nProvisioningParameter adminLogin =\\n    new(nameof(adminLogin), typeof(string))\\n    {\\n        Description = \\u0026#34;The administrator username of the MySQL Flexible Server.\\u0026#34;\\n    };\\ninfra.Add(adminLogin);\\n\\nProvisioningParameter adminPass =\\n    new(nameof(adminPass), typeof(string))\\n    {\\n        Description = \\u0026#34;The administrator password of the MySQL Flexible Server.\\u0026#34;,\\n        IsSecure = true\\n    };\\ninfra.Add(adminPass);\\n\\nMySqlFlexibleServer server =\\n    new(nameof(server), MySqlFlexibleServer.ResourceVersions.V2024_12_30)\\n    {\\n        Sku = new MySqlFlexibleServerSku\\n        {\\n            Name = \\u0026#34;Standard_D2ds_v4\\u0026#34;,\\n            Tier = MySqlFlexibleServerSkuTier.GeneralPurpose\\n        },\\n        AdministratorLogin = adminLogin,\\n        AdministratorLoginPassword = adminPass,\\n        Version = MySqlFlexibleServerVersion.Ver8021,\\n        CreateMode = MySqlFlexibleServerCreateMode.Default,\\n        Storage = new MySqlFlexibleServerStorage\\n        {\\n            StorageSizeInGB = 20\\n        },\\n        Backup = new MySqlFlexibleServerBackupProperties\\n        {\\n            BackupRetentionDays = 7,\\n            GeoRedundantBackup = MySqlFlexibleServerEnableStatusEnum.Disabled\\n        },\\n        HighAvailability = new MySqlFlexibleServerHighAvailability\\n        {\\n            Mode = MySqlFlexibleServerHighAvailabilityMode.Disabled\\n        }\\n    };\\ninfra.Add(server);\\n\\nMySqlFlexibleServerDatabase database =\\n    new(nameof(database), MySqlFlexibleServer.ResourceVersions.V2024_12_30)\\n    {\\n        Parent = server,\\n        Name = \\u0026#34;myDatabase\\u0026#34;,\\n        Charset = \\u0026#34;utf8mb4\\u0026#34;,\\n        Collation = \\u0026#34;utf8mb4_unicode_ci\\u0026#34;\\n    };\\ninfra.Add(database);\\n```\\n```\\n\\n**\\u0026#34;Next steps\\u0026#34; section** — replace the empty heading with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Next steps\\n\\n- [Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n- [Azure Developer CLI documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n- [Azure.Provisioning overview](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md)\\n- [Quickstart: Create an Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n1. **Create the sample test file** at CreateMySqlFlexibleServer()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:MySqlFlexibleServerBasic\\n            Infrastructure infra = new();\\n\\n            ProvisioningParameter adminLogin =\\n                new(nameof(adminLogin), typeof(string))\\n                {\\n                    Description = \\u0026#34;The administrator username of the MySQL Flexible Server.\\u0026#34;\\n                };\\n            infra.Add(adminLogin);\\n\\n            ProvisioningParameter adminPass =\\n                new(nameof(adminPass), typeof(string))\\n                {\\n                    Description = \\u0026#34;The administrator password of the MySQL Flexible Server.\\u0026#34;,\\n                    IsSecure = true\\n                };\\n            infra.Add(adminPass);\\n\\n            MySqlFlexibleServer server =\\n                new(nameof(server), MySqlFlexibleServer.ResourceVersions.V2024_12_30)\\n                {\\n                    Sku = new MySqlFlexibleServerSku\\n                    {\\n                        Name = \\u0026#34;Standard_D2ds_v4\\u0026#34;,\\n                        Tier = MySqlFlexibleServerSkuTier.GeneralPurpose\\n                    },\\n        },\\n                    Backup = new MySqlFlexibleServerBackupProperties\\n                    {\\n                        BackupRetentionDays = 7,\\n                        GeoRedundantBackup = MySqlFlexibleServerEnableStatusEnum.Disabled\\n                    },\\n                    HighAvailability = new MySqlFlexibleServerHighAvailability\\n                    {\\n                        Mode = MySqlFlexibleServerHighAvailabilityMode.Disabled\\n                    }\\n                };\\n            infra.Add(server);\\n\\n            MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase database =\\n                new(nameof(database), MySqlFlexibleServer.ResourceVersions.V2024_12_30)\\n                {\\n                    Parent = server,\\n                    Name = \\u0026#34;myDatabase\\u0026#34;,\\n                    Charset = \\u0026#34;utf8mb4\\u0026#34;,\\n                    Collation = \\u0026#34;utf8mb4_unicode_ci\\u0026#34;\\n                };\\n            infra.Add(database);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n2. **Add the snippet placeholder** in `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md` under the `## Examples` The package provides declarative provisioning support for Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Servers and includes the following generated resource types:\\n\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServer`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServerBackup` / `MySqlFlexibleServerBackupV2`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance`\\n- `AdvancedThreatProtection`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServersPrivateEndpointConnection`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServersCapability`\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **\\u0026#34;Authenticate the Client\\u0026#34; section is empty** — no content; comparable packages like `Azure.Provisioning.PostgreSql` also leave this blank, but for a first release targeting developers it should at minimum note that no client authentication is required (Bicep/ARM output is passed to `azd`)\\n- **\\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section is empty** — no code showing how to create a `MySqlFlexibleServer`; the analogous `Azure.Provisioning.PostgreSql` README includes a full working example with SKU, version, admin credentials, storage, backup, and high availability configuration\\n- **\\u0026#34;Next steps\\u0026#34; section is empty** — no links to Azure MySQL documentation, quickstart templates, or related packages\\n- **No sample test file** — `tests/` directory contains only the `.csproj`; there is no `Samples/` subdirectory with a snippet-backed test, so the README examples cannot be kept in sync with the snippet tooling\\n- **Key concepts section lacks resource inventory** — does not mention which resource types the package exposes or their relationships (e.g., `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase` and `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule` are child resources of `MySqlFlexibleServer`)\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md` — edit\\n\\nReplace the empty `### Authenticate the Client`, `## Key concepts`, `## Examples`, and `## Next steps` sections with the content shown below.\\n\\n**\\u0026#34;Authenticate the Client\\u0026#34; section** — replace the empty heading block with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Authenticate the Client\\n\\nNo runtime client authentication is needed for this library. `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` generates Bicep/ARM templates that are deployed to Azure using the [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) Credentials are handled at deployment time by `azd`, not by this library.\\n```\\n\\n**\\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section** — replace the existing paragraph with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThis library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using .NET. You can then use `azd` to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain Bicep or ARM templates.\\n\\n### Available resource types\\n\\n| Type | ARM resource |\\n|------|-------------|\\n| `MySqlFlexibleServer` | `Microsoft.DBforMySQL/flexibleServers` |\\n| `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase` | `.../databases` |\\n| `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule` | `.../firewallRules` |\\n| `MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator` | `.../administrators` |\\n| `MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration` | `.../configurations` |\\n| `MySqlFlexibleServerBackup` / `MySqlFlexibleServerBackupV2` | `.../backups` |\\n| `MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance` | `.../maintenances` |\\n| `AdvancedThreatProtection` | `.../advancedThreatProtectionSettings` |\\n| `MySqlFlexibleServersPrivateEndpointConnection` | `.../privateEndpointConnections` |\\n```\\n\\n**\\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section** — replace the empty heading with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Create a MySQL Flexible Server\\n\\n```\\nInfrastructure infra = new();\\n\\nProvisioningParameter adminLogin =\\n    new(nameof(adminLogin), typeof(string))\\n    {\\n        Description = \\u0026#34;The administrator username of the MySQL Flexible Server.\\u0026#34;\\n    };\\ninfra.Add(adminLogin);\\n\\nProvisioningParameter adminPass =\\n    new(nameof(adminPass), typeof(string))\\n    {\\n        Description = \\u0026#34;The administrator password of the MySQL Flexible Server.\\u0026#34;,\\n        IsSecure = true\\n    };\\ninfra.Add(adminPass);\\n\\nMySqlFlexibleServer server =\\n    new(nameof(server), MySqlFlexibleServer.ResourceVersions.V2024_12_30)\\n    {\\n        Sku = new MySqlFlexibleServerSku\\n        {\\n            Name = \\u0026#34;Standard_D2ds_v4\\u0026#34;,\\n            Tier = MySqlFlexibleServerSkuTier.GeneralPurpose\\n        },\\n        AdministratorLogin = adminLogin,\\n        AdministratorLoginPassword = adminPass,\\n        Version = MySqlFlexibleServerVersion.Ver8021,\\n        CreateMode = MySqlFlexibleServerCreateMode.Default,\\n        Storage = new MySqlFlexibleServerStorage\\n        {\\n            StorageSizeInGB = 20\\n        },\\n        Backup = new MySqlFlexibleServerBackupProperties\\n        {\\n            BackupRetentionDays = 7,\\n            GeoRedundantBackup = MySqlFlexibleServerEnableStatusEnum.Disabled\\n        },\\n        HighAvailability = new MySqlFlexibleServerHighAvailability\\n        {\\n            Mode =
